meta_title: For Freelancers: user onboarding to Solve slow development speed (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome slow development speed. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Speed Up Development: Master User Onboarding for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop wasting time on repetitive onboarding. Learn how to automate user flows using Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver high-quality SaaS apps faster.
content.answer_block: High-velocity development requires eliminating manual setup. By implementing standardized, automated user onboarding, you reduce churn and technical debt. Utilizing Antigravity to scaffold logic and Claude Code to execute structural changes allows you to deploy production-ready flows in minutes rather than days, directly boosting your agency's bottom line.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking on your client's launch date, and your development velocity has hit a wall. Every hour spent manually configuring user authentication, welcome states, and database seeding is an hour stolen from high-impact feature development. You are building in the trenches, where efficiency is the difference between a profitable project and a scope-creep nightmare.

The secret to scaling as a solo developer isn't working harder; it's architecting systems that do the heavy lifting for you. By leveraging AI-driven orchestration to automate onboarding, you turn a tedious architectural necessity into a repeatable, high-speed asset. It is time to stop coding from scratch and start orchestrating your success.

steps.title: Define Onboarding Schema
steps.description: Establish a robust database schema that handles user state transitions. This prevents fragmented user data and ensures scalability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a scalable PostgreSQL schema for a SaaS user onboarding flow, including state management for 'onboarding_complete', 'profile_setup', and 'tutorial_seen'. Include RLS policies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file schema.sql --task 'Apply the generated schema and verify migrations'

steps.title: Automate Authentication Hooks
steps.description: Trigger onboarding flows immediately upon initial sign-up to ensure zero-friction entry.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript serverless function for Auth0/Clerk webhooks that initializes a new user record in the DB and sets the initial onboarding_state to 'started'.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --command 'npx prisma generate && npx prisma db push'

steps.title: Implement State-Driven UI
steps.description: Create a frontend controller that conditionally renders onboarding components based on the user's DB state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React/Next.js hook 'useOnboarding' that fetches current onboarding state and provides methods to update progress via API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file hooks/useOnboarding.ts --instructions 'Implement logic to handle loading states and API error handling'

steps.title: Build Progress Persistence
steps.description: Ensure users can resume their onboarding flow if they refresh the page or lose internet connectivity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Develop a lightweight middleware to intercept requests and redirect users to the onboarding route if their onboarding_state is incomplete.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Integrate middleware into the Next.js router'

steps.title: Final QA and Stress Testing
steps.description: Simulate edge cases like interrupted flows and database latency to ensure a seamless experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vitest test suite that mocks a user flow from 'new' to 'onboarding_complete', verifying every state transition.

pro_tips: Always seed your local development environment with a 'fake user' onboarding state to test UI edge cases without manual registration.
Use a state machine library like XState for complex onboarding flows to prevent 'spaghetti' logic in your components.
Implement a 'skip onboarding' button as a hidden feature flag for power users during your testing phase to save time.
Keep your onboarding API endpoints strictly decoupled from your main application logic to allow for rapid, independent iterations.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to store your project-specific coding standards, ensuring AI output consistently matches your style.
content.next_steps: After implementing the onboarding flow, focus on integrating analytics to track where users drop off during the setup process, then use the same AI-driven approach to optimize those specific steps.
content.conversion_block: Stop building the same boilerplate for every client. With the right AI-first stack, you can cut your development time by 60% and focus on what actually drives revenue. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
